project.name: fitbase
project.description: FitBase is an exercise library, workout builder, and training log — a buildless single-page app deployed on webface.cloud (fitbase.webface.cloud) with a PocketBase backend, seeded with a 1,324-exercise MIT-licensed dataset.

nodes.name: AGENTS.md
nodes.summary: Platform playbook for agents working on this webface.cloud app: the PocketBase backend already exists at the same origin, hard rules (never build a server, same-origin SDK, escape user content, stay buildless), SDK patterns, and deploy-by-git-push verification.

nodes.name: CLAUDE.md
nodes.summary: Claude Code copy of AGENTS.md — identical webface.cloud platform instructions (buildless frontend, existing PocketBase backend, same-origin SDK, hard rules) so the assistant loads them automatically per session.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Project overview for FitBase (exercise library, workout builder, training log): documents the MIT exercises-dataset vs Gym visual media licensing split, the three PocketBase collections with access rules, the seed script, and the buildless stack.

nodes.name: app.css
nodes.summary: Single stylesheet for the whole SPA: top bar and nav, exercise card grid, filter chips, workout rows, session set-entry rows, history accordion, modal overlay, and toast styling.

nodes.name: index.html
nodes.summary: Single-page shell for FitBase: OG/Twitter meta for the live URL, hash-route nav (Library/Workouts/History), empty #view/#overlay/#toast containers, the Gym visual attribution footer, and script tags loading the vendored PocketBase SDK and app.js.

nodes.name: .understandignore
nodes.summary: One-line ignore list telling the understand-anything analyzer which paths to skip when scanning this repo.

nodes.name: app.js
nodes.summary: The entire FitBase SPA in one vanilla-JS file: same-origin PocketBase client, hash router over five views (library, workouts, history, signin, session), client-side exercise catalog with search/filter, workout CRUD with an inline editor, a live session runner, and session history — all rendered as escaped template strings with global onclick handlers.
nodes.languageNotes: Buildless pattern: no modules or bundler — functions are global so inline onclick attributes in template-string HTML can reach them; every DB string passes through esc() before DOM insertion.

nodes.name: route
nodes.summary: Hash-based router: parses location.hash into a route name and argument, highlights the active nav link, refreshes the auth box, and invokes the matching render function with error fallback.

nodes.name: renderAuth
nodes.summary: Renders the sign-in/sign-up form view, redirecting straight to the library when the visitor is already authenticated.

nodes.name: doAuth
nodes.summary: Form submit handler for auth: creates a users record on sign-up then calls authWithPassword, surfacing PocketBase errors via toast and routing to the library on success.

nodes.name: renderLibrary
nodes.summary: Main library view: loads the full lightweight exercise catalog, derives category/equipment/target filter options from the data, renders the search bar and chips, and wires filter listeners that repaint the grid.

nodes.name: paintGrid
nodes.summary: Paints the paginated exercise card grid from the filtered catalog, hotlinking CDN thumbnails and wiring each card to openDetail; includes a load-more style pager.

nodes.name: openDetail
nodes.summary: Fetches (and caches) the full exercise record, then shows a modal with the animated GIF, muscle metadata, Gym visual attribution, a 10-language instruction selector, and add-to-workout action.

nodes.name: renderWorkouts
nodes.summary: Workouts list view (auth-gated): shows each saved workout with exercise count and Edit/Start/Delete actions, delegating to the editor when a workout id is in the route.

nodes.name: renderWorkoutEditor
nodes.summary: Inline editor for one workout: fetches the record and renders each item row with sets×reps number inputs, reorder arrows, and remove buttons that persist via saveItems.

nodes.name: pickWorkout
nodes.summary: Modal shown from the exercise detail: lists the user's workouts to add the exercise to, plus an inline form to create a new workout with it.

nodes.name: addToWorkout
nodes.summary: Adds an exercise (with default 3×10 sets/reps) to a workout's items JSON after a duplicate check, persists it, and confirms via toast.

nodes.name: startSession
nodes.summary: Initializes an in-memory training session from a workout: builds per-exercise set entries pre-filled to the workout's target sets and reps, then routes to the session view.

nodes.name: renderSession
nodes.summary: Live session runner view: per exercise, an editable set table (reps, weight, delete, add-set) plus a form-check link back to the detail modal, with notes, discard, and finish controls.

nodes.name: finishSession
nodes.summary: Finalizes the session: strips empty sets, refuses to save an all-empty session, creates the owner-scoped sessions record with notes, clears state, and routes to history.

nodes.name: renderHistory
nodes.summary: History view (auth-gated): lists past sessions newest-first with computed total sets and volume, expandable to per-exercise set breakdowns and notes.

nodes.name: DESIGN.md
nodes.summary: Design record for FitBase (2026-07-19): scope (library/builder/log, no charts in v1), the jsDelivr-hotlinked Gym visual media plan and license path, the client-side ~200KB catalog decision, and owner-scoped PocketBase API rules.

nodes.name: pocketbase.umd.js
nodes.summary: Vendored minified PocketBase JS SDK (UMD build) exposing the global PocketBase constructor that app.js uses for auth, CRUD, and the localStorage auth store — vendored so the app stays buildless with no npm dependency.
nodes.languageNotes: Minified single-line UMD bundle; treated as an opaque vendored artifact rather than project code.

nodes.name: setup_fitbase.py
nodes.summary: One-shot backend provisioning script run with PB superuser credentials: authenticates against the live PocketBase, creates the exercises/workouts/sessions collections with owner-scoped rules, enables the batch API, and seeds the 1,324-exercise dataset.
nodes.languageNotes: Stdlib-only Python (urllib) so it runs anywhere without pip installs; idempotent via an ensure-collection existence check.

nodes.name: req
nodes.summary: Minimal JSON HTTP helper over urllib: sends a method/path/body request with optional Authorization token to the PocketBase API and returns (status, parsed JSON), converting HTTPError responses into the same shape.

nodes.name: ensure_collection
nodes.summary: Idempotently creates a PocketBase collection: skips if the name already exists, POSTs the schema payload otherwise, and exits the script on any non-200 response.

layers.name: Frontend SPA
layers.description: The buildless single-page app: the index.html shell with hash-route nav, the entire app logic in app.js (router, library, workout builder, session runner, history), and the single stylesheet.

layers.name: Data Access SDK
layers.description: The vendored PocketBase UMD SDK that gives the SPA same-origin auth, CRUD, and localStorage session persistence without any npm dependency.

layers.name: Backend Provisioning & Tooling
layers.description: One-shot superuser script that creates the exercises/workouts/sessions PocketBase collections with owner-scoped rules and seeds the 1,324-exercise dataset, plus the analyzer ignore config.

layers.name: Documentation
layers.description: Project docs: the README with the MIT-data/Gym-visual-media licensing split, the DESIGN.md decision record, and the AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md webface.cloud platform playbooks.

tour.title: Project Overview
tour.description: Start with the README to learn what FitBase is: an exercise library, workout builder, and training log, live at fitbase.webface.cloud. It documents the two things you must understand before touching anything: the three PocketBase collections (exercises, workouts, sessions) with their access rules, and the licensing split — the 1,324-exercise dataset is MIT, but the GIFs and thumbnails are Gym visual property hotlinked via jsDelivr and must be licensed before any commercial release.

tour.title: The Platform Contract
tour.description: Before reading any code, understand where it runs. AGENTS.md (mirrored by CLAUDE.md for Claude Code) is the webface.cloud playbook: the PocketBase backend already exists at the same origin, so this repo is frontend-only — never add a server, always connect with new PocketBase('/'), and deploy by pushing to master. These hard rules explain every architectural choice you'll see in the next steps.

tour.title: Design Decisions
tour.description: The design record from 2026-07-19 captures why the app looks the way it does: the v1 scope is library, builder, and log with no charts, and the media strategy is jsDelivr hotlinking with attribution as a prototype stopgap until a Gym visual license is purchased. Reading this now means the trade-offs you encounter in the code will register as decisions, not accidents.

tour.title: The App Shell
tour.description: index.html is the single entry point and the root of the dependency graph — it loads the stylesheet, the vendored PocketBase SDK, and app.js, and nothing else. It provides the OG/Twitter meta for the live URL, the hash-route nav (Library, Workouts, History), and three empty containers — #view, #overlay, #toast — that app.js fills at runtime. Everything you see in the browser is rendered into these mount points.
tour.languageLesson: In a buildless SPA there is no bundler: plain <script> tags define load order, so the SDK must load before app.js. Hash-based routing (#/library) works without any server config because the fragment never leaves the browser.

tour.title: The Data Access Layer
tour.description: pocketbase.umd.js is the vendored, minified PocketBase JS SDK — the app's entire data layer in one file with zero npm dependencies. It exposes a global PocketBase constructor that app.js uses for auth, CRUD against the three collections, and automatic session persistence in localStorage. Vendoring it keeps the repo buildless per the platform rules from Step 2.
tour.languageLesson: UMD (Universal Module Definition) builds detect their environment: with no module system present, they attach the export to window, which is exactly what a buildless page needs. Vendoring the file also pins the SDK version — no lockfile required.

tour.title: The Entire App in One File
tour.description: app.js is the heart of FitBase — the highest fan-in node in the graph, referenced by every doc and the setup script. It creates the same-origin PocketBase client and runs a hash router over five views: sign-in, library (paintGrid, openDetail), workout builder (renderWorkoutEditor, addToWorkout), session runner (startSession, finishSession), and history. Notice how each view is just a render function writing into the #view container from Step 4, with all state living in PocketBase records.
tour.languageLesson: Vanilla-JS SPAs replace a framework with two primitives: a hashchange listener that dispatches to render functions, and template strings building DOM. The discipline that makes this safe is escaping every database string before insertion — user content is always untrusted.

tour.title: One Stylesheet for Everything
tour.description: app.css styles the whole SPA: the top bar and nav, the exercise card grid, filter chips, workout rows, session set-entry rows, and the history accordion. One flat stylesheet mirrors the one-file app structure — each view rendered by app.js has a corresponding section here, so UI changes touch exactly two files.

tour.title: Provisioning the Backend
tour.description: The frontend assumes the exercises, workouts, and sessions collections already exist — scripts/setup_fitbase.py is what creates them. Run once with PB superuser credentials, it authenticates against the live PocketBase, builds each collection with the owner-scoped access rules from Step 1's table, enables the batch API, and seeds all 1,324 exercise records. This closes the loop: it is the only piece of the project that touches the backend's schema, everything else consumes it.
tour.languageLesson: Provisioning scripts should be idempotent: ensure_collection checks before creating, so re-running the script converges to the same state instead of failing on duplicates. Seeding via PocketBase's batch API turns 1,324 record inserts into a handful of HTTP requests.
